metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that all four health-band fields named for `coverage.metrics` have upstream counters/data sources in the implementation, and explicitly scope the tracking instrumentation for `verify-drop rate` and `INDETERMINATE rate` which currently have no recording path.
findings.finding: The plan asserts four named health-band fields will be 'emitted on coverage.metrics' (`advisory-vs-blocking ratio`, `findings/run`, `verify-drop rate`, `INDETERMINATE rate`), but the actual `coverage.metrics` dict (confirmed at `gate_dispatch.py:269-283`) contains none of these fields, and the upstream data for `verify-drop rate` and `INDETERMINATE rate` is not tracked anywhere in `gate_dispatch.py` or the orchestrator — no `verify_drop` counter and no INDETERMINATE outcome counter exist in the codebase.
findings.location: AC #2 / What section (#10) — 'health-band metrics emitted on coverage.metrics'
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope section, enumerate the new per-run counters that must be added to `gate_dispatch.py` (or the orchestrator) to feed each of the four metrics: e.g., an INDETERMINATE-outcome counter on the verdict, a verify-drop counter when Pass-2 discards a finding, and a per-run advisory/blocking tally — each feeding into the existing `coverage['metrics']` dict.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify a concrete 'non-trivial diff' threshold for the low-grounding note (e.g. diff exceeds N changed lines or M tokens, or touches more than K files), so the deterministic condition is unambiguous to implement.
findings.finding: The 'low-grounding observational note' for agentic passes that make zero tool calls (#10) lacks a definition of 'non-trivial diff', leaving the condition either always-firing (on every diff) or never-firing depending on how the implementer interprets it. This is a mechanism gap: the deterministic condition is incompletely specified.
findings.location: What / Grounding-effort proxy + health telemetry (#10)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define initial concrete thresholds for the approach-viability note triggers: e.g. 'surviving blocking+advisory findings with priority ≥ 0.8 exceeds N' and 'Pass-2 drop fraction exceeds P%', with a note that these are v1 starting points subject to calibration.
findings.finding: The approach-viability note (#12) trigger conditions — 'unusually high surviving-high-priority count' and 'high Pass-2 drop rate' — have no defined thresholds or calibration reference, making the 'deterministic post-Pass-3 signal' non-deterministic in practice. Without thresholds, the note cannot be implemented as specified.
findings.location: What / Approach-viability note (#12); Acceptance Criteria line 3

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'non-trivial diff' with a concrete threshold (e.g. a minimum changed-line or token count) and specify which coverage.metrics field records per-step tool-call count so the low-grounding detection rule has a co-located data source.
findings.finding: The 'non-trivial diff' predicate used to trigger the low-grounding observational note is undefined — neither a threshold value (line count, file count, token estimate) nor a computation method is specified anywhere in the plan or codebase. Additionally, no 'tool_calls per agentic step' field currently exists in coverage.metrics (the existing fields are det_ms, llm_ms, total_ms, llm_calls, verify_requests, claim_path), so the detection rule cannot be placed co-located with the schema it would read from.
findings.location: What section / AC2: 'Health-band metrics emitted on coverage.metrics; low-grounding note emitted (never blocks).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define each health-band metric's formula and output field name (e.g. `advisory_vs_blocking_ratio = advisory_surfaced / max(1, blocking)`) so the metrics are unambiguously computable from the existing coverage.counts fields.
findings.finding: The four named health-band metrics ('advisory-vs-blocking ratio, findings/run, verify-drop rate, INDETERMINATE rate') are new metric vocabulary to be emitted on coverage.metrics, but their computation formulas and field names are undefined. The plan's AC2 says only 'health-band metrics emitted on coverage.metrics' without specifying how each is calculated from the existing coverage.counts fields (blocking, advisory_surfaced, advisory_overflow, dropped, indeterminate — confirmed in orchestrator.py).
findings.location: What section / AC2: 'Health-band metrics emitted on coverage.metrics; low-grounding note emitted (never blocks).'
findings.suggested_fix: Map the four metrics to coverage.counts fields: advisory_vs_blocking_ratio = advisory_surfaced / max(1, blocking); findings_per_run = blocking + advisory_surfaced + indeterminate; verify_drop_rate = dropped / max(1, blocking + advisory_surfaced + dropped); indeterminate_rate = indeterminate / max(1, blocking + advisory_surfaced + indeterminate + dropped).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before committing to a new manual-ledger ticket convention, verify whether the existing `REVIEW_RESULT` sidecar (`sidecar.py`) + `norm_id` fingerprints + `calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py` + the `no_fire_on_good_cases` eval-spec seeding path already cover the FP-capture and eval-case-drafting use cases, and document what the ledger adds that the sidecar path cannot provide.
findings.finding: The FP-ledger (#7) proposes filing new `bug`-typed rebar tickets as a human-override ledger, then running a compile step to draft eval no-fire cases — without considering that the codebase already has a `REVIEW_RESULT` sidecar (`sidecar.py`) capturing per-finding `norm_id` fingerprints, decisions, and verification attributes, an existing offline calibration script (`docs/experiments/calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py`) described as reconstructing 'per-criterion false-positive / remediation rates', and an existing `plan-review-finder.eval.yaml` with a `no_fire_on_good_cases` scorer already seeded from 'REAL observed false-fires' (`eval/observed-false-positives.md`). The plan risks creating a parallel FP-tracking workflow alongside the already-existing sidecar + calibration + eval-spec pipeline.
findings.location: ## What — FP-ledger convention (#7) / AC: 'fp:code-review ledger convention documented; a compile step drafts eval cases from ledger tickets'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Considered prior art' subsection to ##7 that explicitly addresses: (a) what the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar + offline calibration already provides, (b) what the manual ledger adds (e.g. human-authored root-cause category not inferable from the sidecar), and (c) how the two paths are reconciled (e.g. ledger ticket references the sidecar's norm_id; compile step sources from ledger tickets but uses sidecar data for deduplication). If the sidecar path is sufficient for eval-case drafting, scope the ledger to root-cause annotation only.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to the convention doc a precise structured field schema for the fp:code-review ticket body (at minimum: `criteria_id`, `norm_id` or `finding_fingerprint`, `root_cause_category`, and `plan_excerpt`) that the compile step can parse deterministically to produce an eval-spec `{id, criterion, expect: pass, mode, input}` entry.
findings.finding: The FP-ledger compile step (AC: 'a compile step drafts eval cases from ledger tickets') is under-specified as a mechanism: the plan says ledger entries are rebar tickets tagged fp:code-review, but does not specify what structured fields (e.g. finding text, criteria ID, plan excerpt, root-cause category) the ticket must carry in a parseable form. Without that convention, the compile step has no reliable structured input to generate eval cases from, making the AC unimplementable as stated.
findings.location: What / FP-ledger convention (#7); Acceptance Criteria line 1
findings.suggested_fix: Define the ledger ticket body as a YAML front-matter block (matching the existing eval-spec dataset entry schema: `criterion`, `mode`, `input`) so the compile step is a straightforward YAML extraction rather than natural-language parsing. The `norm_id` from the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar serves as the `fingerprint` join key.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define concrete numeric thresholds for 'non-trivial diff' (e.g. lines-changed ≥ N), 'unusually high surviving-high-priority count' (e.g. ≥ M findings), and 'high Pass-2 drop rate' (e.g. ≥ P%), and add the health-band boundary values to the AC or a linked spec.
findings.finding: The thresholds that govern when the low-grounding note fires ('non-trivial diff') and when the approach-viability note fires ('unusually high surviving-high-priority count', 'high Pass-2 drop rate') are undefined numeric values; an executing agent cannot implement these checks without inventing the thresholds.
findings.location: What / #10 and #12; AC lines 2 and 3
findings.suggested_fix: Add a small threshold table to the plan (or a referenced config file): e.g. non-trivial = diff_lines_changed ≥ 10; viability note fires when surviving_high_priority ≥ 5 OR pass2_drop_rate ≥ 60%; health-band thresholds for advisory-vs-blocking ratio = [0–0.3 = healthy, 0.3–0.6 = warn, >0.6 = alert].

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define initial threshold values as configurable parameters (not hardcoded) and add a brief measurement plan or pilot step (e.g., 'observe distributions over N runs, then set initial band bounds') to derive the health-band and viability-trigger thresholds empirically before or shortly after launch.
findings.finding: The health-band thresholds (advisory-vs-blocking ratio, verify-drop rate, INDETERMINATE rate bounds) and the approach-viability trigger conditions ('unusually high surviving-high-priority count', 'high Pass-2 drop rate') are asserted heuristics with no derivation method and no stated configurability — yet both are explicitly expected to be tuned over time.
findings.location: AC lines 2–3 / What section bullets 2–3
findings.suggested_fix: Add a config block (e.g., in the convention doc or a settings file) for each threshold (health_band.max_indeterminate_rate, viability_note.min_surviving_high_count, etc.) with a note that initial values will be calibrated from the first 4-week run of production data.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add ACs for failure/boundary paths: (a) a condition where the low-grounding note must NOT emit (e.g., trivial diff or adequate tool-call count), asserting it does not appear; (b) a test that the note path never alters the verdict/blocking status even when the emission condition fires; (c) boundary definition and AC for the 'unusually high' threshold used in the viability note signal.
findings.finding: The 'never blocks' invariant for the low-grounding note and the deterministic thresholds for the approach-viability advisory note are new conditional-logic behaviors explicitly guaranteed in the plan, but the ACs only assert the happy-path ('emitted') with no failure-path or boundary-path acceptance criteria — e.g., no criterion for when the note must NOT emit, what happens on a zero-finding run, or how the 'non-trivial diff' boundary is validated.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2 ('low-grounding note emitted (never blocks)') and AC3 ('approach-viability advisory note emitted from a deterministic post-Pass-3 signal')
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Acceptance Criteria: 'Low-grounding note is absent when the agentic pass makes ≥N tool calls on a qualifying diff (N to be defined); the verdict object's blocking fields are identical with and without the note.' and 'Approach-viability note is absent when surviving-high-priority count ≤ X and Pass-2 drop rate ≤ Y (thresholds documented); note never modifies severity or verdict fields.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one concrete validation target or before/after baseline to the ACs (e.g., 'INDETERMINATE rate < X% after N runs', or 'verify-drop rate trend observable over a rolling window') so the reliability improvement is measurable.
findings.finding: No concrete validation mechanism is provided for any AC: the plan emits health-band metrics to coverage.metrics but names no target, threshold, or before/after baseline to confirm the reliability improvement is working.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC #2 ('Health-band metrics emitted on coverage.metrics')
findings.suggested_fix: In AC #2, add a threshold or baseline: e.g., 'health-band metrics emitted; a INDETERMINATE-rate spike >20% over a 7-run rolling window triggers an advisory flag.' For AC #1, add a compile-step validation: 'at least one ledger ticket successfully round-trips to a drafted eval no-fire case in a test run.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the specific file/function where the four new health-band fields will be added to `coverage['metrics']` (currently `gate_dispatch.py:_build_coverage_metrics` or `orchestrator.py:finalize_verdict`) so the AC is verifiable.
findings.finding: The four health-band metric names the plan says will be 'emitted on coverage.metrics' — advisory-vs-blocking ratio, findings/run, verify-drop rate, INDETERMINATE rate — do not exist in the codebase. The current coverage['metrics'] dict (gate_dispatch.py:269-278) holds only {det_ms, llm_ms, total_ms, llm_calls, verify_requests, claim_path}. These are all CREATION targets, not edits to existing symbols.
findings.location: What section (#10) / AC: 'Health-band metrics emitted on coverage.metrics'
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope section, add: 'Add advisory_vs_blocking_ratio, findings_per_run, verify_drop_rate, indeterminate_rate to the metrics dict assembled in gate_dispatch.py:_build_coverage_metrics (and mirrored in orchestrator.py:finalize_verdict for the bespoke path).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify concrete threshold values (or a derivation rule) for 'unusually high surviving-high-priority count' and 'high Pass-2 drop rate', and name the coverage.metrics sub-fields that carry these computed values so the post-Pass-3 rule is truly deterministic.
findings.finding: The approach-viability signal's two trigger conditions — 'unusually high surviving-high-priority count' and 'high Pass-2 drop rate' — are unquantified. No threshold values are given in the plan, and no new coverage.metrics field definition is provided for where these computed values would be stored and read from. Both verdict schemas (plan_review_verdict and code_review_verdict) use `additionalProperties: true` for coverage, so no structural guard enforces the field names.
findings.location: What section / AC3: 'Approach-viability advisory note emitted from a deterministic post-Pass-3 signal.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the closed root-cause-category enum for FP-ledger tickets (e.g. by formally declaring the 11-mode taxonomy from `observed-false-positives.md` as the versioned ledger vocabulary) and specify how the compile step maps categories to eval no-fire case templates.
findings.finding: The 'root-cause category' field required on FP-ledger tickets references an enum vocabulary that is never defined in the plan. The 11-mode FP taxonomy in `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/eval/observed-false-positives.md` is eval-seeding material, not a formally declared closed set for the ledger. An implementer filing or querying ledger tickets has no authoritative value vocabulary to use.
findings.location: What section / AC1: 'fp:code-review ledger convention documented; a compile step drafts eval cases from ledger tickets.'
findings.suggested_fix: Declare the 11 modes from `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/eval/observed-false-positives.md` as the v1 closed enum (e.g. a `LEDGER_ROOT_CAUSE_CATEGORIES` constant co-located with the compile step's query logic), and add a row to `AC1` naming that constant as the source of truth.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a schema/contract doc (or extend the existing coverage.metrics spec) covering the new health-band metric fields and the approach-viability note format so agent consumers have a stable, documented interface.
findings.finding: The health-band metrics added to coverage.metrics and the approach-viability advisory note define new observable contracts for agent consumers, but the plan names no schema/contract update or doc for these signals — only a 'tags/convention doc' for the ledger is called out.
findings.location: Scope section / AC lines 2–3
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'coverage.metrics schema' section to the convention doc (or a separate schema file) listing each new metric name, type, and semantics; similarly document the approach-viability note fields (trigger conditions, output location, advisory-only semantics).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the full RETIRE lifecycle for the FP ledger: specify how the compile step marks tickets as processed, how duplicate fingerprint tickets are deduplicated (e.g., idempotency key = fingerprint hash), and how generated eval cases are versioned and can be retired/superseded.
findings.finding: The FP-ledger (rebar tickets tagged fp:code-review) has no defined RETIRE/cleanup lifecycle: tickets accumulate indefinitely with no specification of whether the compile step marks processed tickets, how superseded eval cases are retired, or how duplicate FP reports for the same fingerprint are deduplicated.
findings.location: What / FP-ledger convention (#7); Acceptance Criteria line 1
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'processed' label transition on rebar tickets after the compile step consumes them; use the finding fingerprint as the primary key so duplicate submissions are collapsed; version eval-case files by fingerprint+category and include a retire/disable mechanism (e.g., a `disabled: true` flag in the eval-case file).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify error handling for the compile step: define timeout, retry/backoff on rebar API failures, skip-and-log behavior for malformed tickets, and emit a metric/alert on compile failure so operators can detect a broken FP-loop.
findings.finding: The periodic compile step that reads rebar tickets and drafts eval cases has no specified error handling: what happens when the rebar API is unavailable, returns partial results, or a ticket is malformed? The plan is silent on retry, timeout, graceful skip, or alerting.
findings.location: What / FP-ledger convention (#7) — 'a periodic compile step turns recurring causes into eval no-fire cases / overlay guards'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a compile-step health metric (e.g., `fp_ledger.compile.status` with labels: success/partial/failed) emitted on each run; on rebar unavailability, log a warning and exit 0 (skip, don't corrupt partial output); validate ticket schema before processing and skip invalid entries with a counter metric.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command or test fixture for each AC: e.g. (AC1) `make compile-fp-ledger` with a sample ticket produces a draft eval-case file; (AC2) inject a synthetic zero-tool-call run and assert the note appears in coverage.metrics output; (AC3) inject a synthetic high-priority-finding set and assert the advisory note is present in verdict assembly output.
findings.finding: None of the three acceptance criteria include a concrete proving command or check that would produce evidence of correct implementation; all three are stated as desired states with no verification mechanism.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria section, all three AC lines

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the target file paths for the FP-ledger convention doc and the compile script (e.g., `docs/fp-ledger.md` and `scripts/compile_fp_ledger.py`), and identify which eval spec file(s) the compile step appends cases to.
findings.finding: The 'fp:code-review' tag, the 'tags/convention doc', and the 'compile step that drafts eval cases from ledger tickets' are all net-new artifacts with no existing counterpart in the codebase. The plan names them as concrete deliverables but does not identify the target file paths (doc location, script location) or which existing eval pipeline the compile step feeds into.
findings.location: What section (#7) / AC: 'fp:code-review ledger convention documented; a compile step drafts eval cases from ledger tickets'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief positive rationale for each of the three mechanisms: why a rebar ticket is the right ledger unit (vs a sidecar annotation), why coverage.metrics is the right emission point (vs a new key), and why a deterministic count threshold is preferred over a Pass-4 coaching move for the viability note.
findings.finding: None of the three plan items state a positive rationale for the chosen approach — why a rebar ticket (vs a sidecar annotation) is the right ledger unit for #7, why coverage.metrics (vs a dedicated health key) is the right emission point for #10, and why a deterministic count threshold (vs a Pass-4 coaching move) is the right mechanism for #12. The absence of rationale is a G6 criterion violation.
findings.location: Why / What sections (all three items #7, #10, #12)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'drafts eval cases' in AC#1 with a specific observable artifact (e.g. 'writes a YAML file to evals/drafts/ containing one candidate no-fire rule per recurring FP cause') so the output format and location are unambiguous.
findings.finding: AC#1 uses the word 'drafts' for the compile step's output ('a compile step drafts eval cases from ledger tickets'), which is effort-framed and does not specify the observable artifact — an agent cannot tell whether a draft means a file on disk, a PR, a YAML snippet, or a human-reviewed intermediary.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC line 1
findings.suggested_fix: Suggested AC#1 rewrite: 'fp:code-review ledger convention documented in CONVENTIONS.md; `make compile-fp-ledger` reads ledger tickets tagged fp:code-review and writes one candidate no-fire rule per recurring cause to evals/drafts/<date>.yaml (human-review gate before promotion to eval suite).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the target function(s) for the approach-viability note insertion (e.g., `orchestrator.finalize_verdict` and `workflow_ops.py`'s verdict assembly op) and specify the output key in the verdict dict where the note will be recorded.
findings.finding: The 'approach-viability advisory note' has no named target file or function in the plan. The plan says 'a small post-decide note in the verdict assembly' but the two verdict-assembly functions (orchestrator.py:finalize_verdict and workflow_ops.py's verdict assembly op) are not explicitly named, and neither currently contains any such note (zero matches for 'approach_viability' or 'surviving.high' across src/).
findings.location: What section (#12) / AC: 'Approach-viability advisory note emitted from a deterministic post-Pass-3 signal'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Consider whether the three sub-items (#7 FP ledger, #10 health telemetry, #12 approach-viability note) should be split into separate child tickets, given each is independently releasable and targets a different subsystem and actor.
findings.finding: The plan bundles three independently valuable, testable, and releasable outcomes — (1) FP ledger convention + tooling, (2) health-band telemetry + grounding note, (3) approach-viability post-Pass-3 note — across distinct subsystems and distinct actors, making the unit a candidate for decomposition.
findings.location: What section (items #7, #10, #12) and Acceptance Criteria (all three ACs)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile AC1 with the 'Manual to start' qualifier in What #7: either restate AC1 as 'compile step convention and schema documented; automation deferred' or explicitly promote the compile step out of the manual-start phase and describe the MVP automation in the plan body.
findings.finding: AC1 states 'a compile step drafts eval cases from ledger tickets' as a verifiable criterion, but the What section qualifies the same feature as 'Manual to start' — leaving ambiguity about whether AC1 is verifying a working automated compile step or a manual procedure, which could send the implementer in two directions.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC1 vs. What section (#7)
findings.suggested_fix: Rewrite AC1 as: 'fp:code-review ledger convention documented (fields: finding, fingerprint, root-cause category); a compile-step procedure defined that can draft eval cases from ledger tickets (manual execution initially, automation noted as follow-on).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document that metric emission is fire-and-forget (catch-and-log on error, never propagate); specify idempotency key or dedup strategy if at-least-once delivery is possible.
findings.finding: Health-band metrics emitted to coverage.metrics have no specified graceful-degradation path if the metrics sink is unavailable; a write failure could propagate as an error into the review pipeline.
findings.location: What / Grounding-effort proxy + health telemetry (#10) — 'health-band metrics emitted on coverage.metrics'
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap all coverage.metrics writes in a try/except that logs the error at WARN level and continues; use a monotonic run-id as an idempotency key on each metric point.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm the 'compile step drafts eval cases' is deferred until the fp:code-review ledger has demonstrated recurring volume (Rule-of-Three), and update AC1 to match the 'Manual to start' intent stated in the What section — e.g. AC1 = 'fp:code-review ledger convention documented; compile step deferred until ≥3 recurring FP root-cause patterns are observed'.
findings.finding: The 'compile step drafts eval cases from ledger tickets' (AC1) is premature automation: the plan's own What section says 'Manual to start', meaning the pipeline (fp:code-review tickets → eval dataset entries) has zero proven recurring runs at the time of delivery. The existing eval spec mechanism (plan-review-finder.eval.yaml) already has a manually-curated false-fire dataset seeded from real FPs; the compile step adds a second, automated path with only one current consumer — failing the YAGNI / Rule-of-Three bar.
findings.location: AC line 1: 'a compile step drafts eval cases from ledger tickets' / What section: 'Manual to start'
findings.suggested_fix: Remove the compile-step deliverable from AC1 (or make it explicitly deferred/stretch). AC1 becomes: 'fp:code-review ledger convention documented (tags, required fields, root-cause vocabulary); the compile step is a named follow-on once ≥3 recurring patterns are logged.' This aligns AC1 with the What section's own 'Manual to start' qualifier.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether 'overlay guards' refers to an existing codebase mechanism (cite the file) or is net-new infrastructure; if net-new, scope it explicitly or remove it from the AC's deliverable.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that recurring FP root-cause patterns will become 'overlay guards' — treating 'overlay guards' as an existing named mechanism in the codebase — but no implementation of an `overlay guards` subsystem is found anywhere in `src/`.
findings.location: What section (#7) — 'recurring causes become eval no-fire cases / overlay guards'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the known consumers of `coverage['metrics']` that may need updating when new health-band keys are added: `sidecar.py:build_payload`, `attest.py` manifest rendering, `_cli/_llm_commands.py` display, and `plan_review_verdict.schema.json`.
findings.finding: The `coverage['metrics']` dict is consumed by at least `sidecar.py:build_payload`, `attest.py` (manifest rendering), and `_cli/_llm_commands.py` (CLI output), all outside `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/`. The plan does not enumerate these consumers, so it is unclear whether adding new keys to the dict requires updating any of those callers (e.g., schema validation, manifest hashing, display formatting).
findings.location: Scope section / AC: 'Health-band metrics emitted on coverage.metrics'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Consumers to audit' subsection under Scope listing these four touch-points and confirming whether each requires a change (likely: schema additive-compatible, sidecar passthrough, CLI display optional).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before specifying new `coverage.metrics` health-band fields, document the current `coverage.metrics` schema (including `verify_requests` and the sidecar's `metrics` sub-field) and confirm which of the four proposed metrics are genuinely absent vs already capturable from existing fields.
findings.finding: The health-band metrics (#10) proposed for `coverage.metrics` (advisory-vs-blocking ratio, findings/run, verify-drop rate, INDETERMINATE rate) partially overlap with metrics already captured in the `REVIEW_RESULT` sidecar's `metrics` field (`det_ms`, `llm_ms`, `total_ms`, `llm_calls`, `claim_path`) and `coverage.metrics.verify_requests` (documented in `plan-review-gate.md`). The plan does not inventory the existing `coverage.metrics` field or the sidecar `metrics` sub-field, so it is unclear whether proposed metrics are net-new fields or duplicates of already-captured data.
findings.location: ## What — Grounding-effort proxy + health telemetry (#10) / AC: 'Health-band metrics emitted on coverage.metrics'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
